focus: maintainability/stabilization: verify renamed documentation-agent surfaces remain understandable and consistent, classify whether P1-001 is localized or broader drift, and prepare synthesis focus

findingDetails.title: Codex agent registry still points at the removed create agent
findingDetails.evidence: Maintainability carry-forward: .codex/agents/markdown.toml identifies the renamed executor as markdown, but .codex/config.toml still declares [agents.create] and config_file = agents/create.toml; resource-map verification omits .codex/config.toml.
findingDetails.recommendation: Rename the Codex registry entry to markdown and point config_file at agents/markdown.toml, or document an intentional legacy alias and add .codex/config.toml verification coverage.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Iteration 004 found broad markdown routing consistency; stale behavior remains localized to the Codex project registry and verification coverage.

ruledOut: broader rename drift across inspected runtime and command surfaces
edgeCases: Reducer-owned findings registry was stale relative to JSONL/strategy before reducer refresh; synthesis should rely on JSONL/strategy until refreshed.

title: Codex agent registry still points at the removed create agent

detail: Maintainability pass classified P1-001 as localized cross-consumer registry drift, not broad rename inconsistency.

reason: Iteration 004 evidence found expected markdown identity/routing across inspected mirrors and create workflow surfaces; only .codex/config.toml remains stale.
